success_surface: Selective cell value filtering using openpyxl with pre-modification inspection and post-modification verification
effective_behaviors: Pre-modification inspection of actual workbook content to discover data types, formulas, and edge cases
Using answer_position metadata to define and constrain processing scope precisely
Selecting openpyxl over pandas for cell-level selective operations requiring formula preservation
Post-operation verification by re-reading modified cells to confirm correct values
Uniform handling of mixed content types (text, numbers, formulas, None values) in single filtering logic
Matching against observed data values rather than assuming instruction text matches cell content exactly
evidence: Agent first loaded workbook and printed column A content (rows 1-20) revealing Comments, numbers, formulas (=IFERROR...), and None values before writing solution
Agent explicitly set start_row=1, end_row=19 based on 'Sheet1'!A1:A19 answer_position to limit processing scope
Solution used openpyxl.load_workbook() instead of pandas.read_excel(), enabling cell-by-cell value access without formula conversion
After wb.save(), agent re-read rows 1-21 and printed repr(cell_value) confirming only 'Comments' strings remain, others are None
Row 18 formula '=IFERROR(#REF!/#REF!,0)' was correctly identified and cleared since it did not equal 'Comments'
Agent matched on capitalized 'Comments' as seen in inspection output, not lowercase 'comments' from instruction

memory_items.title: Inspect Actual Workbook State Before Modification
memory_items.lesson: Always load and examine the real workbook content (sheet names, cell values, formulas, empty cells) before writing transformation logic.
memory_items.generalization: Discovery phase should reveal data types, edge cases, and discrepancies between instructions and actual data to prevent assumptions.

memory_items.title: Scope Processing Using Metadata Constraints
memory_items.lesson: Extract range information from provided metadata (such as answer_position) and enforce it as explicit bounds for all write operations.
memory_items.generalization: Processing limits prevent unintended side effects on untargeted rows, columns, or sheets outside the required range.

memory_items.title: Prefer openpyxl for Granular Cell Filtering
memory_items.lesson: For selective cell value operations where formulas must remain intact and row structure preserved, use openpyxl's cell iteration over pandas batch transforms.
memory_items.generalization: openpyxl supports direct None assignment and conditional logic per cell without losing formula strings or altering workbook layout.

memory_items.title: Validate Changes via Post-Save Verification
memory_items.lesson: After saving modifications, immediately re-load or read the affected range and log results to verify the transformation succeeded as intended.
memory_items.generalization: Verification catches silent failures, confirms condition logic, and provides confidence before marking task complete.

edits.content:
## Trace-Derived Lessons

### Discovery Before Execution
- Load and inspect actual workbook content (sheet names, cell values, formulas, empty cells) before writing transformation logic.
- Identify discrepancies between instruction text and observed data (case sensitivity, unexpected formulas, mixed data types).
- Log key findings to inform subsequent decision-making.

### Range Constraint Enforcement
- Extract range bounds from provided metadata (such as answer_position) and enforce as explicit start/end values.
- Never assume full-column or entire-sheet scope unless explicitly stated.
- Document processing limits in code comments for auditability.

### Tool Selection for Selective Operations
- Prefer openpyxl over pandas when: filtering individual cell values, preserving formulas, maintaining exact row alignment, or handling None/empty states deterministically.
- Pandas batch operations convert formulas to static values; openpyxl retains them.

### Post-Operation Validation
- After saving, re-read the affected range and verify expected values vs. actual values.
- Print summary of kept/cleared/deleted items matching task criteria.
- Use verification output to confirm completion before releasing final file.
